title.en: cross-canonical: group customers by age decade
title.es: cross-canonical: agrupar clientes por década de edad

prompt.en: Given a list of Customer objects each with an int age field, group them into buckets by decade (20s, 30s, 40s, etc.) and return a Map<Integer, List<Customer>> where the key is the decade (2 for ages 20–29, 3 for 30–39, etc.). Which canonical applies?
prompt.es: Dada una lista de objetos Customer cada uno con un campo int age, agrúpalos en cubetas por década (20s, 30s, 40s, etc.) y retorna un Map<Integer, List<Customer>> donde la clave es la década (2 para edades 20–29, 3 para 30–39, etc.). ¿Qué canónico aplica?

label.en: histogram-bucketing — allocate int[12] for age decades 0–11 and count customers per bucket
label.es: histogram-bucketing — asigna int[12] para décadas de edad 0–11 y cuenta clientes por cubeta

label.en: sort-by-rule — sort customers by age/10, then group adjacent elements with the same decade key
label.es: sort-by-rule — ordena clientes por age/10, luego agrupa elementos adyacentes con la misma clave de década

label.en: groupby-key — one pass with computeIfAbsent keyed on customer.getAge() / 10
label.es: groupby-key — un recorrido con computeIfAbsent con clave customer.getAge() / 10

label.en: range-partition — partition customers around age 40 to split young and old groups
label.es: range-partition — particiona clientes alrededor de la edad 40 para separar grupos jóvenes y mayores

explanation.en: The goal is to collect full Customer objects into groups with a computed key (age/10) — groupby-key in one O(n) pass. computeIfAbsent(customer.getAge() / 10, k -> new ArrayList<>()).add(customer) does it directly. Option A (histogram-bucketing) produces counts, not lists of Customer objects — it loses the objects themselves. Option B (sort-by-rule) works but is O(n log n) and does not preserve insertion order within each decade. Option D (range-partition) only creates two partitions, not the multi-decade grouping required.
explanation.es: El objetivo es recopilar objetos Customer completos en grupos con una clave calculada (age/10) — groupby-key en un recorrido O(n). computeIfAbsent(customer.getAge() / 10, k -> new ArrayList<>()).add(customer) lo hace directamente. La opción A (histogram-bucketing) produce conteos, no listas de objetos Customer — pierde los objetos mismos. La opción B (sort-by-rule) funciona pero es O(n log n) y no preserva el orden de inserción dentro de cada década. La opción D (range-partition) solo crea dos particiones, no la agrupación por múltiples décadas requerida.
